steps.title: Use slope-intercept form
steps.content: A linear function can be written as $f(x)=mx+b$, where $m$ is the slope and $b$ is the y-intercept.

steps.title: Substitute the given values
steps.content:
The slope is $\frac{1}{4}$, so $m=\frac{1}{4}$. The point $(0,3)$ is the y-intercept, so $b=3$. Therefore
$$f(x)=\frac{1}{4}x+3.$$
